# NOMAD — MASTER SCRIPT

**Rendered from MASTER DRAFT v0.8. Do not edit this file — all changes go to the draft; this document is re-rendered after.** Bare bones: what the camera sees and what is said. Nothing else.

---

## PROLOGUE

Full frame, no context: PARADISE. A pool at golden hour, too perfect, water like poured glass. A laugh on the soundtrack lands 0.4 seconds late.

One single frame STUTTERS.

**TITLE: NOMAD**

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — DAY (ONE YEAR AGO)

An office at its midpoint — twenty people among visibly recent emptiness. Nobody looks up.

Cairns packs his desk into a printer-paper box, loudly. He gives an exit speech to a floor that isn't listening. Only WILL and JOHN watch. One polite chuckle from somewhere.

The ROBOT BOSS — a monitor on wheels running the boss's warm AI likeness — pulls up. The three of them banter with it; it answers 0.4 seconds late every time. Cairns says the thing meant for the real boss.

CUT TO: the real boss at home, mid golf-sim swing, the office feed propped on a phone he isn't watching.

Back on the floor, the likeness thanks Cairns warmly for the feedback.

## INT. ELEVATOR BANK — CONTINUOUS

Will and John flank Cairns, carrying nothing. John asks the plan. Cairns brags about a SERVICE — "they handle everything, visa, apartment, one fee" — and can't answer a single follow-up. The doors close on him mid-sentence.

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — MOMENTS LATER

The floor already back at work. Cairns' chair, still spinning. On his desk: a branded welcome folder.

**SUPER: ONE YEAR LATER**

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — MORNING

Same floor. Nine people. Motion-sensor lights click on in sequence ahead of Will. He passes dark departments and Cairns' untouched desk — the folder still there, a year of dust on it. At 8:55 the coffee machine brews a full pot. Will pours its only cup.

Somewhere in the dark, the Robot Boss greets empty desks by name.

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — WILL'S DESK — DAY

A queue of faces. Machine verdicts. One button: APPROVE. Click. Click. A streak counter climbs.

A face comes through, machine-verified 98%. Will stops. Replays three seconds — the candidate asked about his childhood, the eyes going nowhere. Will overrides: SYNTHETIC. The system checks. Concedes. His streak resets to zero.

He looks up to tell someone. Eight heads down.

On his monitor: SYSTEM 99.1 / WILL 96.8 — TOP HUMAN PERFORMER.

## INT. WILL'S APARTMENT — NIGHT

Dinner eaten standing over the sink. An HR popup — 34 unused vacation days — dismissed by reflex.

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — WILL'S DESK — DAY

Will's display: CAIRNS — poolside, sun, abundance.

### CAIRNS

You wouldn't believe it here, bro. Everything's two for one. And I'm not just talking about the food — right, ladies?

*(wink wink)*

### TWO GIRLS

¡Sí!

*(giggling)*

### CAIRNS (CONT'D)

You gotta get out here. It's life on crack. Same setup for work — but with bottomless margaritas.

The girls escalate — from decoration, to distraction, to pulling him out of frame.

### CAIRNS (CONT'D)

Anyway — you know I'm tight with Michelle. Say the word, I'll get you set up.

### CAIRNS (CONT'D)

Ladies, ladies — calm your horses. Plenty of papi to go around. Ladies!

They really make him go now.

### CAIRNS (O.S.) (CONT'D)

Love you, buddy!

The screen stays on. The three of them run to the pool. Cairns throws one girl in. Will can't help but watch — jealousy sprouting in his expression.

### JOHN (O.S.)

Is that Cairns? Nice...

Will kills the tab and turns. John, holding two beers.

## INT. DEAD SALES DEPARTMENT — MINI-PUTT COURSE — LATER

One round by phone-light. Hole 7 uses the old CEO's office door as a ramp.

### JOHN

Doesn't sound too bad... You know I've never even left the state?

They let that sit. Then — who's Michelle? The Service was a company; since when does it have a first name? Neither of them knows. They shrug it off.

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — WILL'S DESK — DAY

The river dashboard: the machine's decisions scrolling too fast to read. Will spots a fraud he's certain of. INTERVENE. His screen dims; a coaching module auto-plays — "Trusting Your Tools," a cheerful presenter who blinks at perfectly regular intervals. In the corner, the system processes his case without him. Correct verdict. No acknowledgment.

## INT. DEAD SALES DEPARTMENT — DAY

Mini-putt with John. Mid-round, Will checks the time — and just leaves. Mid-afternoon. Nobody stops him. Nobody notices.

## INT. WILL'S APARTMENT — NIGHT

The pool clip, rewatched with the sound off. He closes the laptop. Opens it. Looks up the Service.

## INT. SERVICE STOREFRONT — DAY

An office park. The Service's local office sits between a vape shop and a notary. Brochure rack, folding chairs, a looping paradise reel above a dying pothos. A bored coordinator checks Will in like a dentist's office.

Intake: INSERT — a scanner's green line crossing his passport. Face capture — look left, look right, read this sentence aloud. Will jokes that he does this for a living. The coordinator, flat: then you know it's painless. 0.4 seconds.

"Your relocation coordinator will be in touch."

## INT. WILL'S CAR — PARKING LOT — CONTINUOUS

The email arrives before he's out of the lot. MICHELLE — chirpy, template-warm: *So excited to start your journey!!* A scheduling link.

## INT. WILL'S APARTMENT — NIGHT

Michelle's "welcome preview": an automated advertisement starring WILL — his face, his walk, composited poolside into Cairns' paradise. "See yourself there." He watches himself live the ad, delighted. INSERT — a checkbox filling: CONFIRM MY DETAILS. He says his name for the personalization. Says it again, excited. Grinning.

## INT. WILL'S APARTMENT — LATER

The tour quiz: an app films him walking his apartment, narrating furniture preferences. For confirmation it plays his own face back to him — and for half a second his own eyes look wrong. He blames the compression.

## INT. OFFICE SPACE — DAY

A bloodless company email: THE FLOOR IS CLOSING — full remote by end of October.

John's answer: the last day in office will be the MINI-PUTT CHAMPIONSHIP. He starts the bracket that day.

## INT. WILL'S APARTMENT — NIGHT

John helps Will pack. He extracts one promise: be back for it. He finds Will's putter, tapes WILL on the grip, and keeps it — so you don't have an excuse. Will promises, half-listening.

## EXT./INT. MEXICO CITY — SEQUENCE

The ad comes true. The apartment beats the photos. The fridge is stocked with his exact brands. A rooftop workday; Will closes the laptop mid-refresh. No clocks anywhere.

He texts Cairns on landing — "next week bro, big project." Invites him to a party — "just left town."

A rooftop party recreates the frame of Cairns' call — someone thrown in the pool. Will catches himself inside the ad, and toasts it. He films it for Cairns. The reply is warm, instant — and postpones again.

He walks through one conversation between locals — subtitled for us, not for him — about the building where "the American who's never home" lives. The building itself: background, unnoticed.

## INT. WILL'S CDMX APARTMENT / INT. DEAD OFFICE — NIGHT (INTERCUT)

Will calls JOHN. Mini-putt updates from the dark floor; Will poolside, pretending he isn't lonely at a party. John, helpful, without knowing: weird — Cairns liked my bracket post yesterday. He's clearly online.

## INT. WILL'S CDMX APARTMENT — NIGHT

The usual poolside frame. Cairns, still selling, still "next week bro." Will, easy and warm, asks a nothing question — a private memory only the two of them hold.

The answer comes back correct but transcribed. Facts right. Eyes going nowhere.

Four seconds of Will fully awake. And the call does not end. It continues, pleasantly, for a full further beat — Will performing normalcy, managing a warm goodnight. *Love you bro* comes back. 0.4 late.

He closes the laptop and sits very still in the best apartment he's ever had.

## INT. WILL'S CDMX APARTMENT — NIGHT — THE SCRUB

Desk lamp. Notes. Side-by-sides: old real videos of Cairns — years of them, birthdays, dumb ones — frame-stepped against the new calls. Timestamps across time zones that don't add up. The laugh isolated, waveformed, measured: 0.4.

Mid-scrub, the phone lights up. MICHELLE: his final residency appointment, moved up. Tomorrow. A time — the first specific time anyone has given him in this country. An address he half-recognizes.

## EXT. CAIRNS' BUILDING — DAY

Across the street. Ordinary. He watches it a while.

## INT. CAIRNS' BUILDING — DAY

A day early. Reception expects him tomorrow; nobody manages what he sees. The service stairs.

Upstairs: nothing. No set, no pool. An ordinary empty floor — chairs, capture rigs like the storefront's, racks of humming servers.

Through a doorway: the RENDER FARM. An operator types a line; a poolside evening assembles itself in seconds — Cairns' pool, water that has never been wet. On another screen a girl who does not exist is thrown, laughing, into it; a slider drags her laugh warmer. On a third: familiar faces — likenesses at work, attending standups. Whole lives, versioned and running.

On one screen, mid-generation: WILL'S OWN welcome ad, being refined. Him, poolside, saying things he's never said.

Then, from the next row — WILL'S OWN LAUGH. The real one. He follows his own voice.

The worst screen in the building. A live session, running unattended: CAIRNS' LIKENESS on a call with WILL'S LIKENESS. Poolside together. Riffing. Their in-jokes. The friendship, automated, running fine without either of them. Version numbers in the corner of the frame.

Will slips out. An operator notices him in a doorway reflection, and does not care.

## THE DOUBLE RITUAL — INTERCUT

**EXT. CAIRNS' BUILDING — DAY.** From below. Monolithic. It fills the frame. Will walks in.

**INT. DEAD SALES DEPARTMENT — DAY.** LAST DAY IN THE OFFICE. The floor half-dismantled — desks tagged for pickup, cables coiled, the coffee machine unplugged. A paper banner goes up. The bracket on the whiteboard: one name crossed out, rewritten smaller — WILL (REMOTE?).

**INT. CLEAN ROOM — DAY.** A chair, good light, liveness rigs he recognizes from his own firm. Forms already complete. INSERT — a checkbox, already filled: ☑ REFERENCE RETENTION. Behind him, a wall of small numbered lockers, most tagged. One tag readable: a first name and a version number.

**INT. DEAD SALES DEPARTMENT.** John in a homemade referee shirt by hole 7. Two beers. Will's putter — WILL taped on the grip — leans against the hole, reserved.

**INT. CLEAN ROOM.** The door closes politely.

**INT. DEAD SALES DEPARTMENT.** The banner settles.

**INT. CAIRNS' BUILDING — HALLWAY.** Will's phone, face-up on a tray. Michelle's chat thread archiving itself. The screen sleeps.

**INT. DEAD SALES DEPARTMENT.** John's phone BUZZES.

A message from Will — cadence perfect, warmth slightly too on-message: not gonna make it back for the championship bro... but mexico is crazy, you gotta come out HERE.

We never see the screen. Only John's face, lit by it — one flicker of disappointment, then delight.

John smiles. Starts typing back.

HOLD ON JOHN — the putter in frame.

One frame STUTTERS.

CUT TO BLACK.

**THE END**
